steps.action: Bar is a Turkish folk dance from the Eastern Ana with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Bar is a Turkish folk dance from the Eastern Ana with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: The word bar is from the Armenian word "Պար" (ba
steps.text: The word bar is from the Armenian word "Պար" (bar) which means dance.

steps.action: With their structure and formation, they are the
steps.text: With their structure and formation, they are the dances performed by groups in the open.

steps.action: The characteristic of their formation is that th
steps.text: The characteristic of their formation is that they are performed side-by-side, hand, shoulder and arm-in-arm.

steps.action: The principal instruments of bar dances are davu
steps.text: The principal instruments of bar dances are davul and zurna (shrill pipe).

steps.action: Aksak 98 measures which are the most characteris
steps.text: Aksak 98 measures which are the most characteristic measures, in particular, of the Turkish folk music are applied with extremely different and interesting structures in this dance.

steps.action: They are spread, in general, all over the region
steps.text: They are spread, in general, all over the region of Eastern Anatolia, especially in Artvin, Ardahan, Erzurum, Bayburt, Ağrı, Kars, and Erzincan provinces.

steps.action: Woman and man bars are different from one anothe
steps.text: Woman and man bars are different from one another.

steps.action: The dominant measures in bars are 58 and 98.
steps.text: The dominant measures in bars are 58 and 98.

steps.action: Occasionally measures of 68 and 128 are also use
steps.text: Occasionally measures of 68 and 128 are also used.

steps.action: A famous song of Bar is called 'Atabarı'.
steps.text: A famous song of Bar is called 'Atabarı'.
